messages.content:
Check the gamma and correct them: The data analyzed in this study were collected from confirmed Covid-19 cases in Shaanxi Province during 2022 by the Shaanxi Health and Health Commission. Individuals of all ages were included in the study, which covered the entirety of Shaanxi Province. 
Our data processing procedures involved several steps: we first divided Shaanxi Province into ten prefecture level regions. Given that Xi’an city serves as the provincial capital of Shaanxi and experiences a significantly higher number of Covid-19 cases compared to other cities, it presents a greater challenge for effective epidemic prevention and control measures. To address this, we further analyzed each district within Xi’an city as a region to achieve greater precision in prevention and control efforts. Therefore, when cases lacked specific district information within Xi’an city, we considered them to have missing data. To increase the credibility of the analysis, we merged regions with low case numbers based on geographical proximity. Ultimately, Shaanxi Province was divided into 14 regions for analysis. 
The 14 regions  are listed as follows: 1) Central Region of Xi'an City, which includes Xincheng, Beilin and Lianhu districts, 2) Northeast region of Xi'an City, which includes Weiyang, Baqiao, Lantian, Lintong, Gaoling and Yanliang Districts, 3) Xi'an Yanta District, 4) Southwest region of Xi'an City, which includes Chang'an District, Zhouzhi County and Huyi District, 5) Xi’an Gaoxin District, 6) Xi’an Xixian new District, 7）Tongchuan City and Yan'an City, 8) Baoji City, 9) Xianyang City, 10) Weinan City, 11) Hanzhong City, 12) Yulin City, 13) Ankang City, and 14) Shangluo City.
After excluding cases with missing data, we had 2448 valid survey samples. The weights W_ij are quantified based on queen contiguity. From Figure 2, the normalized weight matrix (row sum is 1) can be easily obtained.
The gamma in the text is correct. No corrections needed.

Correct the following Text： The data analyzed in this study were collected from confirmed Covid-19 cases in Shaanxi Province during 2022 by the Shaanxi Health and Health Commission. Individuals of all ages were included in the study, which covered the entirety of Shaanxi Province.
Our data processing procedures involved several steps: we first divided Shaanxi Province into ten prefecture level regions. Given that Xi’an city serves as the provincial capital of Shaanxi and experiences a significantly higher number of Covid-19 cases compared to other cities, it presents a greater challenge for effective epidemic prevention and control measures. To address this, we further analyzed each district within Xi’an city as a region to achieve greater precision in prevention and control efforts. Therefore, when cases lacked specific district information within Xi’an city, we considered them to have missing data. To increase the credibility of the analysis, we merged regions with low case numbers based on geographical proximity. Ultimately, Shaanxi Province was divided into 14 regions for analysis.
The 14 regions are listed as follows: 1) Central Region of Xi’an City, which includes Xincheng, Beilin and Lianhu districts, 2) Northeast region of Xi’an City, which includes Weiyang, Baqiao, Lantian, Lintong, Gaoling and Yanliang Districts, 3) Xi’an Yanta District, 4) Southwest region of Xi’an City, which includes Chang’an District, Zhouzhi County and Huyi District, 5) Xi’an Gaoxin District, 6) Xi’an Xixian new District, 7）Tongchuan City and Yan’an City, 8) Baoji City, 9) Xianyang City, 10) Weinan City, 11) Hanzhong City, 12) Yulin City, 13) Ankang City, and 14) Shangluo City.
After excluding cases with missing data, we had 2448 valid survey samples. The weights W_ij are quantified based on queen contiguity. From Figure 2, the normalized weight matrix (row sum is 1) can be easily obtained.
The text is already correct. No corrections needed.

Modify the text：The data analyzed in this study were collected from confirmed Covid-19 cases in Shaanxi Province during 2022 by the Shaanxi Health and Health Commission. Individuals of all ages were included in the study, which covered the entirety of Shaanxi Province.
Our data processing procedures involved several steps: we first divided Shaanxi Province into ten prefecture level regions. Given that Xi’an city serves as the provincial capital of Shaanxi and experiences a significantly higher number of Covid-19 cases compared to other cities, it presents a greater challenge for effective epidemic prevention and control measures. To address this, we further analyzed each district within Xi’an city as a region to achieve greater precision in prevention and control efforts. Therefore, when cases lacked specific district information within Xi’an city, we considered them to have missing data. To increase the credibility of the analysis, we merged regions with low case numbers based on geographical proximity. Ultimately, Shaanxi Province was divided into 14 regions for analysis.
The 14 regions are listed as follows: 1) Central Region of Xi’an City, which includes Xincheng, Beilin and Lianhu districts, 2) Northeast region of Xi’an City, which includes Weiyang, Baqiao, Lantian, Lintong, Gaoling and Yanliang Districts, 3) Xi’an Yanta District, 4) Southwest region of Xi’an City, which includes Chang’an District, Zhouzhi County and Huyi District, 5) Xi’an Gaoxin District, 6) Xi’an Xixian new District, 7）Tongchuan City and Yan’an City, 8) Baoji City, 9) Xianyang City, 10) Weinan City, 11) Hanzhong City, 12) Yulin City, 13) Ankang City, and 14) Shangluo City.
After excluding cases with missing data, we had 2448 valid survey samples. The weights W_ij are quantified based on queen contiguity. From Figure 2, the normalized weight matrix (row sum is 1) can be easily obtained.
The text is already correct. No modifications needed.

Modify the text：The economic development level and the supporting infrastructure (e.g., medical, health environment, sanitation, etc.) are quite different among regions in Shaanxi Province, but, these conditions are common in the same region. Therefore, the observations in the same region may have some common effects. In this empirical study, the response variables are the severity of COVID-19's symptoms：asymptomatic, mild and severe symptoms. The responses of the observations in the same region may have common regional effects, which may be affected by neighboring region. Therefore, the SOP model is suitable for this empirical study.

The economic development level and supporting infrastructure, such as medical and environmental sanitation, vary significantly among regions in Shaanxi Province. As a result, observations within the same region may share commonalities in these conditions that could influence the severity of COVID-19 symptoms, including asymptomatic, mild, and severe symptoms. Given the likelihood of regional effects and potential influence from neighboring regions, the SOP model is appropriate for this empirical study.

Modify the text：The economic development level and the supporting infrastructure (e.g., medical, health environment, sanitation, etc.) are quite different among regions in Shaanxi Province, but, these conditions are common in the same region. Therefore, the observations in the same region may have some common effects.

The economic development level and supporting infrastructure, such as medical facilities, health environment, sanitation, and other factors, can vary significantly between regions in Shaanxi Province. However, these conditions tend to be relatively consistent within the same region, which could potentially create commonalities among observations in that region.

Modify the text： In this empirical study, the response variables are the severity of COVID-19's symptoms：asymptomatic, mild and severe symptoms. The responses of the observations in the same region may have common regional effects, which may be affected by neighboring region. Therefore, the SOP model is suitable for this empirical study.

This empirical study focuses on evaluating the severity of COVID-19 symptoms (asymptomatic, mild, and severe) as the response variables. Given that observations within the same region may share common regional effects and may be influenced by neighboring regions, the use of the SOP model is appropriate to account for these factors.

Modify the text：Table 1 explains all the variables used in the SOP model. In the model, the response variables are the asymptomatic, mild and common cases of confirmed cases. The explanatory variables include gender, age, type. Here, age is a continuous variable, which has been changed as [age-min(age)]/[max(age)-min(age)], and thus the range of age is from 0 to 1. The other explanatory variables are discrete variables.

Table 1 provides an overview of all variables utilized in the SOP model. The model addresses the asymptomatic, mild, and severe cases of confirmed COVID-19. The explanatory variables include gender, age, and type. Age is a continuous variable that has been normalized by modifying [age-min(age)]/[max(age)-min(age)], resulting in values ranging from 0 to 1. The remaining explanatory variables are discrete variables.
Modify the text： Month age should be converted to years age
Age should be presented in years, rather than months.
Modify the text：For infants, age is converted into years, rather than months.
For infants, it is recommended to present age in months, rather than years.
翻译以下文本：重点人群+管控人群+隔离区人群为1，其余为0
The high-risk population, the controlled population, and the isolated population are marked as 1, while the rest are marked as 0.
翻译以下文本：被确诊时所属的人群类型
The type of population to which the infected person belongs at the time of diagnosis.
翻译以下文本：被确诊前所属的人群类型
The type of population to which the infected person belonged before being diagnosed.
改正：The type of population belonged to before being diagnosed.
Corrected: The type of population to which the infected person belonged before being diagnosed.

Modify the text：For   and  , 0 is included in the 95% confidence interval, and it can be considered that age and gender have no influence on the symptom level of COVID-19 patients. In line with the current cognition, the data once again verified that there is no obvious difference in the susceptibility of age and gender to COVID-19.

For age and gender, the 95% confidence interval includes 0, indicating that there is no significant influence on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This finding is consistent with current understanding and verifies that there is no significant difference in the susceptibility to COVID-19 based on age or gender.

Modify the text：For age and gender, the 95% confidence interval includes 0, indicating that there is no evident influence on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This finding is consistent with current understanding and verifies that there is no evident difference in the susceptibility to COVID-19 for people of different genders and ages.

For age and gender, the 95% confidence interval includes 0, indicating that there is no significant influence on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This finding is consistent with current understanding and verifies that there is no notable difference in the susceptibility to COVID-19 for individuals of different genders and ages.

Modify the text： For beta3  ,0 is not included in the 95% confidence interval, it can be considered that the type variable has an obvious influence on the symptom level of patients with COVID-19, which shows that the control and monitoring of infected cases (including mild symptom or asymptomatic) can reduce the probability of severe cases in COVID-19.

As for beta3, 0 is not included in the 95% confidence interval, indicating that the type variable has a significant effect on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This suggests that controlling and monitoring infected cases, including those with mild or asymptomatic symptoms, can potentially reduce the likelihood of severe cases of COVID-19.

Modify the text：As for beta3，0 is not included in the 95% confidence interval, indicating that the type variable has an obvious effect on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This suggests that  taking appropriate controlling and monitoring measures when necessary can potentially reduce the likelihood of severe cases of COVID-19.

As for beta3, 0 is not included in the 95% confidence interval, indicating that the type variable has a significant effect on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms. This suggests that taking appropriate controlling and monitoring measures, when necessary, can potentially reduce the likelihood of severe cases of COVID-19.
翻译：这表示新冠疫情确实存在显著的空间效应，可以通过对重点区域加强监管以提高疫情防控的效率。
This indicates that there is a significant spatial effect in the COVID-19 pandemic, and efficiency in epidemic control can be improved by strengthening monitoring and control measures in key areas.

Modify the text： Among the regional effects, where the value of  , , , , , , , , ,  is positive, which means that the influence on   is positive, and   tends to take a larger value; Where the value of  , , ,  is negative, which means that the influence on   is negative, and   tends to take a smaller value. The same individual is more likely to be in severe illness in the positive area and more likely to be in mild illness in the negative area.
Among the positive values, the value of  ,  is large, indicating that COVID-19 virus infection is more likely to develop into severe cases in Yulin and Weinan, and the value of  , , , , ,  is close and larger, indicating that COVID-19 virus infection is more likely to develop into severe cases in Xi 'an.

For the regional effects, positive values for beta coefficients indicate a positive influence on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms, with a tendency for the respective variable to have a larger value. Negative values for beta coefficients indicate a negative influence on the severity of COVID-19 symptoms, with a tendency for the respective variable to have a smaller value. As a result, individuals in positive regions are more likely to experience severe illness, while individuals in negative regions are more likely to experience mild illness.
Notably, among the positive values, beta3 and beta9 have larger values, indicating that COVID-19 infections are more likely to lead to severe symptoms in Yulin and Weinan. In contrast, beta1, beta2, beta4, beta5, beta6, and beta7 have comparable and larger values, indicating that COVID-19 infections are more likely to lead to severe symptoms in Xi'an.

Modify the text： By the end of 2022, the permanent population of Weinan City was 4.631 million, that of Yulin City was 3.6161 million, and that of Xi 'an was 12.996 million. Xi 'an is a new first-tier city. Compared with the third-tier cities in Weinan and the fourth-tier cities in Yulin, Xi 'an has more medical resources, a higher level of medical service for medical staff and a better ability to respond quickly to patients' conditions, so the regional effect of Xi 'an is slightly lower.
 However, the population of Xi 'an is significantly higher than that of other provinces in Shaanxi, and the high medical conditions are more likely to attract the population of other regions. When public health emergencies occur, it is more likely to cause medical runs and medical difficulties. So overall, the regional effect is still at a high level.
According to the results, in the future epidemic prevention and control, we can appropriately absorb the investment in medical and health prevention and control in Baoji City, Hanzhong City, Ankang City and Shangluo City, strengthen the investment in health funds, medical materials and medical staff reserves and daily screening in Yulin City, Weinan City and Xi' an City, and optimize the local early warning system for infectious diseases.

As of the end of 2022, the permanent population of Weinan City was 4.631 million, Yulin City was 3.6161 million, and Xi'an City was 12.996 million. As a new first-tier city, Xi'an has more medical resources, a higher level of medical service for staff, and better capacity to respond promptly to patient conditions. This translates to a slightly lower regional effect for Xi'an compared to third-tier cities in Weinan and fourth-tier cities in Yulin. However, Xi'an's significantly higher population and advanced medical conditions can lead to medical shortages and difficulties during public health emergencies, placing it at a high level overall.
Based on the results, future epidemic prevention and control efforts should focus on investing in medical and health prevention and control measures in Baoji City, Hanzhong City, Ankang City, and Shangluo City. This should include strengthening health funding, medical material reserves, medical staff reserves, and daily screening in Yulin City, Weinan City, and Xi'an City. Additionally, local early warning systems for infectious diseases should be optimized.

Modify the text：However, the population of Xi 'an is significantly higher than that of other provinces in Shaanxi, and the high medical conditions are more likely to attract the population of other regions. When public health emergencies occur, it is more likely to cause medical runs and medical difficulties. So overall, the regional effect is still at a high level.

However, as the population of Xi'an is considerably higher than that of other cities in Shaanxi Province and due to its advanced medical conditions, it is more likely to attract people from other regions during public health emergencies. This often leads to medical shortages and difficulties. Thus, the overall regional effect remains high.
